hypothesis: Incorporating explicit electronegativity difference tensors between shell 1 and shell 2 elements as an additional conditioning signal in the latent diffusion model will improve adsorption energy prediction accuracy and generation fidelity for multi-component HEA surfaces beyond what adsorbate identity, scalar adsorption energy, and element list alone can achieve, because inter-shell electronegativity gradients directly modulate charge transfer at the active site and thus encode ligand effects that the current conditioning scheme captures only implicitly.
